title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
After leaving Mrs. Luton, a detective ponders the name "Rosemary," which Mrs. Luton explained was chosen by her sister, while "Sheila" was named after their mother. 

Colin Lamb travels through the streets near Covent Garden to a crowded, dimly lit bookstore near the British Museum owned by Mr. Solomon. Lamb is researching seaweed and seeks books on ocean biology and fossils. He navigates through narrow aisles and various floors, passing through a "treasure" storage area on the second floor before reaching the third floor. There, he enters a room where he encounters a middle-aged woman, whom he calls "Nanny," who informs him that a man named Mr. Collins was unhappy with his progress.

Lamb enters a smoke-filled room to meet Colonel Beck, a group leader. Beck expresses frustration regarding the lack of progress in Lamb's investigation into "Wilbraham Crescent." Lamb reports that he has been investigating houses on New Moon Street and mentions a discovery: a murder occurred on Wilbraham Crescent, though the victim's identity remains unknown because a business card found in their pocket contained a fake name and address. Lamb requests permission to investigate specific individuals, identifying a house called "Diana's Cottage" located at 20 Wilbraham Crescent in Crofton, ten miles from Portbury. He explains that the cottage is inhabited by a Mrs. Hemm, who spends her time caring for eighteen cats.
